articles.title: Summer School Holiday 2026: Check Delhi-NCR, UP, Bihar, Rajasthan & Other States School Holidays Amid Severe Heatwave; Details - The Daily Jagran
articles.full_text:
Summer Vacation heatwave conditions across India have forced several state governments to announce summer vacations in schools. Rising temperatures and hot winds have started affecting daily life, prompting authorities to prioritize students’ health and safety. States including Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Rajasthan, Telangana and Andhra Pradesh have either declared summer holidays or changed school timings due to the intense heat.
In some states, schools have already been closed earlier than scheduled as temperatures continue to rise. Education departments in northern states have released official summer vacation schedules, while some districts have temporarily shortened school hours.
Rajasthan
In Rajasthan, after a weather alert, the education department has revised school timings to 7:30 AM to 12:00 PM in all districts, including Jaipur. Summer vacations in the state will begin on May 17, 2026, and continue until June 20, 2026. District collectors may also declare early holidays in areas severely affected by heatwave conditions.
Delhi-NCR
In Delhi, the Directorate of Education has announced summer vacations in both government and private schools. Schools will remain closed from May 11 to June 30, 2026, and will reopen on July 1, 2026.
Uttar Pradesh and Bihar
In Uttar Pradesh, extreme heat has led to changes in school timings in several districts, including Prayagraj. Summer vacations are expected to start around May 20 and may continue until mid-June.
Meanwhile, in Bihar, school timings have been shortened to 11:30 AM due to the heat. According to the academic calendar, summer vacations will run from June 1 to June 20, 2026.

States Where Schools Are Already Closed
Several states had already declared summer holidays earlier due to severe heatwave conditions:
-Telangana and Andhra Pradesh: Vacations began April 24, 2026, with schools reopening in mid-June.
-Odisha: Summer holidays started April 27, 2026.
-West Bengal: Many districts declared holidays from April 22, 2026.
-Chhattisgarh: The state has one of the longest breaks, from April 20 to June 15, 2026.
State-wise Summer Vacation Schedule 2026:
Authorities have advised parents and students to regularly check official updates from local education departments or school administrations, as vacation dates may change depending on the intensity of the heatwave.

articles.title: Rajasthan's Weather Relief: Storms Cool Down Scorching Heat - Devdiscourse
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Rajasthan's Weather Relief: Storms Cool Down Scorching Heat
Rajasthan experienced rain and dust storms thanks to a western disturbance, alleviating intense heat. The Meteorological Centre in Jaipur reported light to moderate rain and gusty winds in isolated areas. Srimadhopur in Sikar district saw the highest rainfall of 48 mm. Temperatures dropped significantly across the state.
Rajasthan saw a reprieve from blistering heat as several regions experienced rain and dust storms due to a western disturbance, according to the Meteorological Centre in Jaipur.
The state recorded light to moderate rainfall, accompanied by thunderstorms and gusty winds, in isolated locations within the last 24 hours.
Srimadhopur in the Sikar district reported the highest rainfall, measuring 48 mm, and brought maximum temperatures down to between 39 and 41 degrees Celsius across much of the state.
(With inputs from agencies.)
